Items.Title: 一种用于在 React-Native 中显示加密货币图表的 API | One API to show crypto charts in react-native
Items.Description:
什雷·维杰瓦尔吉亚

发表于 6 月 27 日

如何在反应本机移动应用程序中开发加密货币价格图表。

引擎盖下

对于新人来说，我现在已经写了很多关于react-native的文章，因为我正在开发一个web3非托管钱包应用程序。

现在有趣的是，无论我在应用程序中开发什么，我都可以分享有关它的文章，因为它也让其他人变得容易。

让我们开始吧，每个 web3 钱包应用程序都列出了加密货币或代币以及价格和图表。

为了渲染我们需要的图表

  • 当然，我们将使用react-native npm 库来处理图表。

    我正在使用react-native-chart-kit，你可以使用任何你觉得舒服的东西。

    继续安装库并渲染基本演示图表，如下是代码。

     yarn add react-native-chart-kit

    使用演示数据渲染演示图表

    <LineChart data={{ labels: ["January", "February", "March", "April", "May", "June"], datasets: [ { data: [ Math.random() * 100, Math.random() * 100, Math.random() * 100, Math.random() * 100, Math.random() * 100, Math.random() * 100 ] } ] }} width={Dimensions.get("window").width} // from react-native height={220} yAxisLabel="$" yAxisSuffix="k" yAxisInterval={1} // optional, defaults to 1 chartConfig={{ backgroundColor: "#e26a00", backgroundGradientFrom: "#fb8c00", backgroundGradientTo: "#ffa726", decimalPlaces: 2, // optional, defaults to 2dp color: (opacity = 1) => `rgba(255, 255, 255, ${opacity})`, labelColor: (opacity = 1) => `rgba(255, 255, 255, ${opacity})`, style: { borderRadius: 16 }, propsForDots: { r: "6", strokeWidth: "2", stroke: "#ffa726" } }} bezier style={{ marginVertical: 8, borderRadius: 16 }} />

    只需2步即可在react-native中渲染折线图

    定制化

    这也不是火箭科学。

    它提供了大量的道具和样式方法来设计图表的样式。

    看看上面的chartConfig props和propsforDots等等，你就会明白我们可以多么轻松地定制它。

    按照文档添加更多自定义内容。

    其他图表

    是的，它确实提供了其他类型的图表，如下所示

    • 折线图
    • 条形图
    • 饼形图
    • 贡献图
    • 进度表等。

    加密代币列表
    对于 web3 钱包应用程序，我们首先需要呈现代币列表以及美元价格。

    为此，我们使用第三方 API 来获取代币及其各自的价格。

    我正在使用 Coingecko API 来显示代币列表并获取不同时间间隔的每个代币的价格，例如

    • 一天
    • 一周 ＆
    • 一个月这是API文档

    获取代币价格

    export const fetchCoinPricesViaNameAndDays = async (coinName, days) => { const response = await axios.get( `https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/coins/${coinName}/market_chart?vs_currency=usd&days=${days}`, ); return response.data.prices; };

    这将返回时区和价格数组，我们可以简单地将其传递到折线图中以呈现我们的图表。

    该方法采用代币名称，即代币 ID 和我们想要获取价格的时间间隔。

    例如，如果我们提供的代币名称为“比特币”，天数为“1”，它将返回 24 小时时间间隔的价格数组。

    检查下面的链接以更好地理解

    api.coingecko.com/api/v3/coins/bitcoin/...

    图表

    比特币一日图

    比特币 7 天走势图

    同样，您可以更改币名如eth或doge或shibadoge来获取相应的图表数据。

    结论

    要在反应原生中开发图表，我们需要执行以下步骤

    • 使用react-native-chart-kit显示图表
    • 获取多个时间间隔的数据
    • 获取代币和时间间隔价格
    • 将价格数组传递给图表以渲染图表

    继续发展Shrey

shrey vijayvargiya

Posted on Jun 27

 

How to develop cryptocurrency price charts in the react-native mobile app.

Under the Hood

For the newcomers, I’ve been writing a lot on react-native nowadays because I am developing a web3 non-custodial wallet app.

Now the interesting thing is whatever I am developing in the app I can share the article on it as it makes things easy for others also.

Charts in React Native

Let’s begin the story, Every web3 wallet app has cryptocurrencies or tokens listed along with the prices and chart.

In order to render the chart we need

  • y-axis meaning the price in USD
  • x-axis meaning the time Using these 2 data points we can render the chart easily.

Of course, we will be using react-native npm libraries to deal with charts.

I am using react-native-chart-kit and you can use whatsoever you are comfortable with.

react-native-chart-kit npm module

Go ahead and install the library and render the basic demo chart as below is the code.

yarn add react-native-chart-kit

Render the demo chart using demo data

<LineChart
    data={{
      labels: ["January", "February", "March", "April", "May", "June"],
      datasets: [
        {
          data: [
            Math.random() * 100,
            Math.random() * 100,
            Math.random() * 100,
            Math.random() * 100,
            Math.random() * 100,
            Math.random() * 100
          ]
        }
      ]
    }}
    width={Dimensions.get("window").width} // from react-native
    height={220}
    yAxisLabel="$"
    yAxisSuffix="k"
    yAxisInterval={1} // optional, defaults to 1
    chartConfig={{
      backgroundColor: "#e26a00",
      backgroundGradientFrom: "#fb8c00",
      backgroundGradientTo: "#ffa726",
      decimalPlaces: 2, // optional, defaults to 2dp
      color: (opacity = 1) => `rgba(255, 255, 255, ${opacity})`,
      labelColor: (opacity = 1) => `rgba(255, 255, 255, ${opacity})`,
      style: {
        borderRadius: 16
      },
      propsForDots: {
        r: "6",
        strokeWidth: "2",
        stroke: "#ffa726"
      }
    }}
    bezier
    style={{
      marginVertical: 8,
      borderRadius: 16
    }}
  />

Only 2 steps to render the line chart in react-native

Customisation

This is not rocket science either.

It provides tonnes of props and styling ways to style your chart.

See the above chartConfig props and propsforDots and so on and you will understand how easily we can customise it.

Follow the docs to add more customisation.

Other Charts

Yes it does provide other types of charts as follows

  • Line chart
  • Bar chart
  • Pie Chart
  • Contribution Charts
  • Progress Chart and so on.

Crypto Tokens List
For the web3 wallet app, we first need to render the list of tokens along with the price in USD.

For that, we are using third-party API to fetch the tokens and their respective prices.

I am using Coingecko APIs to show the list of tokens and to fetch the prices of each token for different time intervals such as

  • One day
  • One week &
  • One month Here is the API documentation

Fetching tokens prices 

export const fetchCoinPricesViaNameAndDays = async (coinName, days) => {
  const response = await axios.get(
    `https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/coins/${coinName}/market_chart?vs_currency=usd&days=${days}`,
  );
  return response.data.prices;
};

This will return the timezone and prices array that we can simply pass in the line chart to render our chart.

The method takes the token name meaning the ID of the token and the time interval we want to fetch the prices.

For example, if we provide the token name as bitcoin and days as 1, it will return an array of prices of the 24-hour time interval.

Check the link below to understand better

api.coingecko.com/api/v3/coins/bitcoin/…

Charts

One day Bitcoin chart

7 days Bitcoin chart

One month Bitcoin chart

3 Months Bitcoin chart

6 Months Bitcoin chart

One-year Bitcoin chart

Similarly, you can change the coin name such as eth or doge or shibadoge to get the corresponding charts data.

Conclusion

TO develop charts in react-native we need to do the following steps

  • Show the chart using react-native-chart-kit
  • Fetch data for multiple time intervals
  • Fetch tokens and time interval prices
  • Pass the price array to the chart to render the chart

Keep developing Shrey

Items.Title: Web 开发人员的面试问题 | Interview Questions for a Web Developer
Items.Description:
安基塔·贾达夫

发表于 6 月 27 日

求职面试可能很困难，特别是如果您是入门级 Web 开发人员。 但是，为常见问题做好准备可能会帮助您避免任何困难。 如果您已经知道潜在的问题和答案，这将增强您对面试的信心，最终您将在面试中留下积极的印象。

在这篇文章中，我们将介绍一些最常见的 Web 开发人员面试问题及其答案。

  1. 是什么促使您从事网络开发职业？
    如果您是一名新的 Web 开发人员，那么您可能会经常被问到这个问题。 无论您的故事是什么，找到一种有趣的方式来展示您对编码的热情，以及这将如何为您未来的雇主带来好处。

  2. 客户端脚本和服务器端脚本有什么区别？
    客户端脚本是指在用户浏览器上运行的脚本。 这些脚本与网页内容一起下载到用户的计算机，并在那里执行。 客户端脚本语言的示例包括 JavaScript、HTML 和 CSS。 客户端脚本通常用于在网页上创建动态效果和用户交互，例如弹出窗口和动画。

另一方面，服务器端脚本是指在 Web 服务器上运行的脚本。 这些脚本动态生成网页，并将生成的 HTML 发送到用户的浏览器进行显示。 服务器端脚本语言的示例包括 PHP、Ruby on Rails 和 Python。 服务器端脚本通常用于创建需要更复杂处理的 Web 应用程序，例如电子商务网站。

  1. 您精通哪些编程语言？
    要回答这个问题，您需要在参加任何面试之前精通任何一种编程语言，并且每当被问到这个问题时，请毫不犹豫地谈论您最有信心的语言。

  2. 描述您使用 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 等前端开发技术的经验。
    要成为一名 Web 开发人员，您需要掌握 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 技能，因为这些是任何 Web 应用程序开发过程的重要语言。 描述您最近完成的任何小型或大型项目，并自信地谈论它。

  3. 解释 HTML 和 XHTML 之间的区别 – 简短回答。 什么是网络 API？ 解释一下将第三方 API 集成到您的 Web 应用程序中的过程？
    本质上，它是不同软件系统交换数据和功能的一种方式，而不需要直接访问彼此的代码。 它们通常使用标准 HTTP 请求和响应，并且可以使用 JavaScript、Python 和 Ruby 等编程语言进行访问。

将第三方 API 集成到您的 Web 应用程序中的过程：
将第三方 API 集成到 Web 应用程序的过程包括研究 API、获取 API 凭证、测试 API、将 API 集成到应用程序、处理错误、优化性能和监控使用情况。

  1. 解释一下 HTTP 和 HTTPS 之间的区别？ 为什么 HTTPS 对于网络安全很重要？

它是一种明文协议，这意味着服务器和客户端之间发送的数据未加密，因此很容易被攻击者拦截和修改。 另一方面，HTTPS 是 HTTP 的加密版本，它使用 SSL/TLS（安全套接字层/传输层安全性）来加密服务器和客户端之间发送的数据。 为什么 HTTPS 对于网络安全很重要？
它也是搜索引擎的排名信号，对于搜索引擎优化非常重要。

  1. 您在 Web 开发的测试和质量保证方面有什么经验？
    它涉及功能、可用性、安全性和性能测试，以及进行质量检查以识别和解决任何缺陷或问题。 最佳实践包括创建测试计划、使用自动化测试工具、进行用户验收测试、执行安全审核以及持续监控和改进应用程序。

  2. 发送 GET 请求时，数据将作为查询字符串编码在 URL 中，在浏览器的地址栏中可见。 这样可以轻松共享 URL 并为其添加书签。 邮政：

    另一方面，POST 请求用于向服务器提交数据。 发送 POST 请求时，数据在请求正文中发送，该正文在浏览器地址栏中不可见。 解释一下CSS中的盒子模型？
    它由四个部分组成：内容、内边距、边框和边距。 这些组件显示为矩形框，内容在里面，内边距、边框和边距在外面。 盒子模型决定了元素在页面上的大小和位置，设计者可以调整每个组件的值来控制网页的间距和布局。

  3. 什么是响应式设计？ 如何为 Web 应用程序创建响应式设计？
    响应式网页设计是一种网页设计方法，旨在创建能够适应不同屏幕尺寸和设备（例如智能手机、平板电脑和台式电脑）的网站。 它涉及使用灵活网格、响应式图像和媒体查询等技术，根据所使用设备的屏幕尺寸来调整网站的布局和内容。 响应式网页设计的目标是在所有设备上提供无缝且最佳的用户体验，而不需要单独的移动和桌面版本的网站。

如何为 Web 应用程序创建响应式设计？ 通过执行以下步骤，您可以为 Web 应用程序创建响应式设计。 什么是 AJAX？ 它在网络开发中是如何工作的？ 它是一种通过与服务器异步发送和接收数据来创建动态和响应式网页的技术，而无需重新加载整个页面。

通过 AJAX，Web 应用程序可以动态更新内容，而不需要用户手动刷新页面。 它可以提供更无缝、更快速的用户体验，并减少客户端和服务器之间传输的数据量。 有哪些方法可以优化 Web 应用程序的性能？

优化 Web 应用程序性能的一些方法包括： 最大限度地减少 HTTP 请求 缓存 压缩文件 优化图像 最大限度地减少代码 使用内容交付网络 (CDN)

  2. 解释一下数据库 MySQL 或 MongoDB？
    它将数据存储在表中并强制它们之间的关系。 它使用SQL（结构化查询语言）来查询和管理数据，这使得它成为具有复杂数据关系的应用程序的不错选择。

另一方面，MongoDB 是一个 NoSQL 数据库管理系统，它使用 BSON（二进制 JSON）编码以基于文档的格式存储数据。 它不强制数据之间的关系，这使其成为具有灵活数据结构的应用程序的不错选择。 它还具有强大的查询功能，并支持地理空间索引和聚合等高级功能。

  1. 调试 Web 应用程序的技术有哪些？

什么是 ReactJS 或 AngularJS？ 它使用虚拟 DOM（文档对象模型）有效地呈现对 UI 的更改，从而提高性能。

它使用双向数据绑定方法和基于组件的体系结构来构建用户界面。

  1. 解释一下 CSS 预处理器，例如 SASS 或 LESS？ 这些预处理器允许开发人员使用变量、函数和 mixin 等功能，以更有组织、更高效的方式编写 CSS。 这使得维护和修改 Web 应用程序的样式变得更加容易，因为可以在一处进行更改并应用于整个项目。

    总体而言，CSS 预处理器对于希望为其 Web 应用程序编写更高效且可维护的 CSS 代码的 Web 开发人员来说是很有价值的工具。

原文:

Ankita Jadhav

Posted on Jun 27

 

Job Interview can be difficult, specially if you are an entry level Web Developer. However, preparing yourself for frequently asked questions may help you to avoid any difficulties. If you already know the potential questions and answers the it will boost your confidence for the Interview and eventually you will end up making a positive impression in the interview.

In this post we will go through some of the most common interview questions & their answers for web developer.

  1. What made you pursue career in web development ?
    If you are a fresher web developer then you might get asked this question frequently. Whatever your story is, find an interesting way to show your passion for coding, and how that will be of benefit to your prospective employer.

  2. What is the difference between client-side and server-side scripting?
    Client-side scripting refers to scripts that run on the user's browser. These scripts are downloaded to the user's computer along with the web page content, and executed there. Examples of client-side scripting languages include JavaScript, HTML, and CSS. Client-side scripting is often used to create dynamic effects and user interactions on web pages, such as pop-up windows, and animations.

Server-side scripting, on the other hand, refers to scripts that run on the web server. These scripts generate the web page dynamically, and the resulting HTML is sent to the user's browser for display. Examples of server-side scripting languages include PHP, Ruby on Rails, and Python. Server-side scripting is often used for creating web applications that require more complex processing, like e-commerce sites.

  1. What programming languages are you proficient in?
    To answer this question, you need to be proficient in any one programing language before you appear for any interview and whenever this question is asked without hesitation speak about the language that you are most confident in.

  2. Describe your experience with front-end development technologies such as HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
    “Practice makes man perfect” we must have heard this saying many times. To be a web developer you need to master HTML, CSS and JavaScript skills as this are the important languages for any web application development process. Describe any mini or major project that your have completed recently and confidently talk about it.

  3. Explain the difference between HTML and XHTML – Short answer. HTML (Hypertext Markup Language) and XHTML (Extensible Hypertext Markup Language) are both markup languages used to create web pages. XHTML is stricter and more structured than HTML, but it also requires more attention to detail when writing code.

    HTML is more forgiving and easier to learn, but it may not be suitable for creating complex web applications.

  4. What is a web API? Explain the process of integrating a third-party API into your web application?
    A web API (Application Programming Interface) is a set of protocols and tools that allow different software applications to communicate with each other over the internet. Essentially, it is a way for different software systems to exchange data and functionality, without requiring direct access to each other's code. Web APIs are commonly used to enable third-party developers to build applications that integrate with existing systems or services. They typically use standard HTTP requests and responses, and can be accessed using programming languages such as JavaScript, Python, and Ruby.

Process of integrating a third-party API into your web application:
The process of integrating a third-party API into a web application involves researching the API, obtaining API credentials, testing the API, integrating the API into the application, handling errors, optimizing performance, and monitoring usage.

  1. Explain the difference between HTTP and HTTPS? Why is HTTPS important for web security?

HTTP:
HTTP is the standard protocol used for transferring data between a web server and a web client, such as a web browser. It is a clear-text protocol, which means that the data sent between the server and the client is not encrypted, making it susceptible to interception and modification by attackers. HTTP operates on port 80 by default.

HTTPS:
HTTPS, on the other hand, is an encrypted version of HTTP that uses SSL/TLS (Secure Sockets Layer/Transport Layer Security) to encrypt the data sent between the server and the client. HTTPS provides a secure and encrypted connection, which makes it much more difficult for attackers to intercept or modify the data being transmitted. HTTPS operates on port 443 by default.

Why is HTTPS important for web security?
HTTPS is important for web security because it encrypts data being transmitted between a web server and a client, provides data integrity, authentication, and helps prevent man-in-the-middle attacks. It's also a ranking signal for search engines, making it important for search engine optimization.

  1. What is your experience with testing and quality assurance in web development?
    Testing and quality assurance in web development are crucial for ensuring that web applications function as expected and meet user requirements. It involves testing for functionality, usability, security, and performance, as well as conducting quality checks to identify and address any defects or issues. Best practices include creating a testing plan, using automated testing tools, conducting user acceptance testing, performing security audits, and continuously monitoring and improving the application.

  2. What are the differences between a GET and a POST request?

GET: A GET request is used to retrieve data from a server. When a GET request is sent, the data is encoded in the URL as a query string, which is visible in the address bar of the browser. This makes it easy to share and bookmark the URL. GET requests are typically used for retrieving static content, such as HTML, CSS, and JavaScript files. POST:

A POST request, on the other hand, is used to submit data to a server. When a POST request is sent, the data is sent in the request body, which is not visible in the address bar of the browser. POST requests are typically used for submitting form data, uploading files, or performing any action that modifies server-side data.

  1. Explain the box model in CSS?
    The box model in CSS is a concept that defines how elements are displayed on a web page. It consists of four components: content, padding, border, and margin. These components are displayed as a rectangular box, with the content inside and the padding, border, and margin outside. The box model determines how elements are sized and positioned on the page, and designers can adjust the values of each component to control the spacing and layout of their web pages.

  2. What is a responsive design? How to create responsive design for a web application?
    Responsive web design is an approach to web design that aims to create websites that can adapt to different screen sizes and devices, such as smartphones, tablets, and desktop computers. It involves using techniques such as flexible grids, responsive images, and media queries to adjust the layout and content of a website based on the screen size of the device being used. The goal of responsive web design is to provide a seamless and optimal user experience across all devices, without the need for separate mobile and desktop versions of a website.

How to create responsive design for a web application? By following below steps you can create responsive design for web application. • Use a responsive web design framework, such as Bootstrap or Foundation, to save time and effort. • Use flexible grids, which allow you to create layouts that can adjust to different screen sizes. • Use responsive images, media queries, and relative units for font sizes and element sizes.

• Test your design on different devices and screen sizes to ensure that it looks and functions correctly on all devices.

  1. What is AJAX? How does it work in web development? AJAX stands for Asynchronous JavaScript and XML. It is a technique for creating dynamic and responsive web pages by sending and receiving data asynchronously with the server without having to reload the entire page.

    With AJAX, web applications can update content dynamically, without requiring the user to manually refresh the page. It allows for a more seamless and faster user experience, as well as reducing the amount of data transferred between the client and server. AJAX uses JavaScript to make requests to the server and manipulate the content on the page in real-time, and can use various data formats, not just XML.

  2. What are some ways to optimize the performance of a web application?

Some ways to optimize the performance of a web application are: Minimizing HTTP requests Caching Compressing files Optimizing images Minimizing code Using a content delivery network (CDN)

Implementing lazy loading.

  1. What is your experience with server-side languages such as PHP or Node.js?
    Be genuine while answering this question if you have used it then talk a short summary about it. I you have not then tell the interviewing that “I have not got a chance to work on it yet, I am a fast learner and given opportunity I can learn anything”

  2. Explain the databases MySQL or MongoDB?
    MySQL is a relational database management system (RDBMS) that is widely used for web applications. It stores data in tables and enforces relationships between them. It uses SQL (Structured Query Language) for querying and managing the data, which makes it a good choice for applications with complex data relationships.

MongoDB, on the other hand, is a NoSQL database management system that stores data in a document-based format, using BSON (Binary JSON) encoding. It does not enforce relationships between data, making it a good choice for applications with flexible data structures. It also has powerful querying capabilities and supports advanced features like geospatial indexing and aggregation.

  1. What are the techniques of debugging a web application?

Some techniques of debugging a web application are:

• Console logging • Breakpoints • Code review • Error messages • Remote debugging • Unit testing

• Profiling

  1. What is ReactJS or AngularJS? ReactJS: ReactJS, developed by Facebook, is a component-based library for building user interfaces. It uses a virtual DOM (Document Object Model) to efficiently render changes to the UI, resulting in faster performance.

AngularJS:
AngularJS, developed by Google, is a complete framework for building web applications. It uses a two-way data binding approach and a component-based architecture for building user interfaces.

  1. Explain CSS preprocessors such as SASS or LESS? CSS preprocessors such as SASS (Syntactically Awesome Style Sheets) and LESS (Leaner Style Sheets) are tools that extend the capabilities of CSS (Cascading Style Sheets).

These preprocessors allow developers to write CSS in a more organized and efficient way, using features such as variables, functions, and mixins. This makes it easier to maintain and modify the styles of a web application, as changes can be made in one place and applied throughout the entire project.

SASS and LESS both use a syntax that is similar to CSS, with some added features. SASS uses a syntax that is similar to traditional programming languages, with curly braces and semicolons, while LESS uses a more CSS-like syntax with nested rules.

Overall, CSS preprocessors are valuable tools for web developers looking to write more efficient and maintainable CSS code for their web applications.

  1. Can you describe a recent project you worked on and what challenges you faced?
    Again, your genuine nature is really important in the interview so whichever project you have worked on talk about it freely.

  2. What is CMS (content management system)

A CMS (content management system) is a software application that enables users to create, manage, and publish digital content for websites. It provides an easy-to-use interface with features such as editing tools, media management, content organization, and user management, allowing website owners and content creators to publish content without requiring extensive knowledge of coding or web development.

These are just a few examples of the types of technical interview questions that web developers may have to face. Items.Title: 为什么企业需要响应式网页设计？ | Why Do Businesses Need Responsive Web Design ?
Items.Description:
网页设计是指创建和设计网站的视觉布局、用户界面和整体美感的过程。 它涉及结合图形、版式、颜色、图像和交互功能等元素来创建一个引人入胜且用户友好的网站。

企业需要响应式网页设计有几个重要原因：

移动设备使用量：随着移动设备使用量的显着增加，拥有一个响应式网站至关重要。 响应式设计可确保您的网站在各种设备（例如智能手机和平板电脑）上正确显示并正常运行。 这使您能够满足不断增长的移动受众的需求，并在不同的屏幕尺寸上提供无缝的用户体验。

用户体验 (UX)：响应式设计在提供最佳用户体验方面发挥着至关重要的作用。 它允许您的网站适应不同的设备和屏幕分辨率，确保内容易于阅读、图像尺寸适当且导航方便用户使用。 通过提供积极的用户体验，您可以提高客户满意度，并增加用户在您的网站上停留更长时间、探索您的产品以及转化为客户的机会。

搜索引擎优化 (SEO)：响应式网页设计会对您的搜索引擎排名产生积极影响。 像谷歌这样的搜索引擎会在搜索结果中优先考虑适合移动设备的网站，因为它们的目标是为用户提供最佳的用户体验。 拥有一个响应式网站可以通过降低跳出率、提高用户参与度和增强跨设备的网站性能来改善您的 SEO 工作。

成本效益：响应式设计无需为不同设备创建单独的网站，而是可以让您拥有一个可适应不同屏幕尺寸的网站。 这种方法在开发、维护和内容管理方面更具成本效益和效率。 它消除了管理站点的多个版本的需要，并确保跨设备的品牌和内容的一致性。

提高覆盖范围和可访问性：响应式网站使您的内容可供更广泛的受众访问。 通过满足不同设备上的用户的需求，您可以吸引主要使用移动设备或平板电脑进行浏览的潜在客户。 此外，响应式设计也有利于残疾人，因为它支持辅助技术并为所有用户提供包容性的体验。

未来的可扩展性：投资响应式设计可确保您的网站的可扩展性和对未来技术进步的适应性。 随着具有不同屏幕尺寸和分辨率的新设备的出现，您的网站将做好充分准备来适应它们。 这使您免于频繁重新设计和重新开发以跟上不断发展的技术的麻烦。

竞争优势：拥有响应式网站可以为您的企业带来竞争优势。 由于许多用户通过移动设备访问网站，响应式设计可让您从那些可能未针对移动设备查看优化其网站的竞争对手中脱颖而出。 适合移动设备且以用户为中心的网站可以提高您的品牌声誉，并在目标受众中留下积极的印象。

总之，企业需要响应式网页设计来迎合移动受众，提供最佳的用户体验，提高搜索引擎排名，节省成本，增加覆盖范围和可访问性，确保未来的可扩展性，并在数字领域获得竞争优势。

原文:

Web design refers to the process of creating and designing the visual layout, user interface, and overall aesthetic of a website. It involves combining elements such as graphics, typography, colors, images, and interactive features to create an engaging and user-friendly website.

Businesses need responsive web design for several important reasons:

Mobile device usage: With the significant increase in mobile device usage, having a responsive website is crucial. Responsive design ensures that your website displays properly and functions well on various devices, such as smartphones and tablets. This allows you to cater to the growing mobile audience and provide a seamless user experience across different screen sizes.

User experience (UX): Responsive design plays a vital role in delivering an optimal user experience. It allows your website to adapt to different devices and screen resolutions, ensuring that content is easily readable, images are appropriately sized, and navigation is user-friendly. By providing a positive UX, you enhance customer satisfaction and increase the chances of users staying longer on your site, exploring your offerings, and converting into customers.

Search engine optimization (SEO): Responsive web design positively impacts your search engine rankings. Search engines like Google prioritize mobile-friendly websites in their search results, as they aim to deliver the best user experience to their users. Having a responsive site improves your SEO efforts by reducing bounce rates, increasing user engagement, and enhancing site performance across devices.

Cost-effectiveness: Instead of creating separate websites for different devices, a responsive design allows you to have a single website that adjusts to different screen sizes. This approach is more cost-effective and efficient in terms of development, maintenance, and content management. It eliminates the need for managing multiple versions of your site and ensures consistency in branding and content across devices.

Increased reach and accessibility: A responsive website makes your content accessible to a wider audience. By catering to users on different devices, you can reach potential customers who primarily use mobile devices or tablets for browsing. Additionally, responsive design also benefits individuals with disabilities, as it supports assistive technologies and provides an inclusive experience for all users.

Future scalability: Investing in responsive design ensures your website's scalability and adaptability for future technological advancements. As new devices with varying screen sizes and resolutions emerge, your website will be well-prepared to accommodate them. This saves you from the hassle of frequent redesigns and redevelopment to keep up with evolving technologies.

Competitive advantage: Having a responsive website gives your business a competitive edge. With many users accessing websites on mobile devices, a responsive design sets you apart from competitors who may not have optimized their sites for mobile viewing. A mobile-friendly and user-centric website enhances your brand's reputation and creates a positive impression among your target audience.

In summary, businesses need responsive web design to cater to the mobile audience, provide an optimal user experience, improve search engine rankings, save costs, increase reach and accessibility, ensure future scalability, and gain a competitive advantage in the digital landscape.

Items.Description:
借助称为销售智能的创新技术解决方案，销售团队可以发现、分析并更深入地了解潜在客户的前景，同时跟踪重要的购买信号。 销售情报提供了丰富的多样化数据和对客户购买模式的独特见解。

它帮助销售人员识别竞争对手可能忽视的难以捉摸的前景，并检查价格和数量的精确演变。 这一点至关重要，因为销售人员花费大部分时间来确定满足其理想客户档案要求的可行性。 接下来，他们确定这些潜在消费者是否有可能进行购买，并评估投入时间和精力将他们转变为忠实客户的可行性。

销售情报的优势

如何获得销售情报

为您的销售情报工作找到最重要的数据库。 客户关系管理 (CRM) 系统、综合市场研究报告、各种社交媒体平台、备受推崇的行业出版物、竞争对手的在线域以及备受推崇的新闻 API只是获取信息的著名方法的几个例子。

定期检查和评估销售情报工作的有效性。 跟踪和评估关键绩效指标 (KPI)，例如转化率、收入流程、客户满意度和销售周期持续时间。 利用从数据驱动的调查中获得的见解来确定需要改进的领域并优化您的销售工作。

新闻 API 在收集可靠且具有决定性的新闻数据以制定策略和了解市场方面发挥着主导作用。

用于销售情报的新闻 API

如果要在不需要人工干预的情况下获得详细说明任何给定组织或个人的最新新闻和事件的综合仪表板，那么使用工具或平台就变得至关重要。 它是一个非常宝贵的工具，可用于直接从庞大的网络中提取有关任何组织的新闻数据。

这个内容广泛的数据库是根据 20,000 多个新闻来源编制而成的。 可以在 Excel 或 JSON 中快速轻松地访问获取的数据，从而可以轻松地将它们合并到您想要的分析框架或其他应用程序中。

突发新闻： Newsdata 的一些主要广泛功能是向用户提供突发新闻，使他们能够及时了解覆盖 150 多个国家/地区的全球时事动态。

据我们所知， Google News Feed API现已停用，并且请求限制为每天最多 100 篇文章，并且由于没有此限制，数据量也受到限制，因此这里使 NewsData.io 成为 Google News API 的最佳替代方案。

历史新闻：用户可以根据需要提取最长5年的历史新闻数据。 历史重演的行情相当广泛地基于市场行为，跟踪过去的数据有助于用户更广泛地了解市场的本质。

新闻分析：审视数据是了解市场本质的重要方面。 结论
销售额和营业额是每个组织都渴望和追求的目标。 每个组织和在其中工作的人员都有不同的最终目标和动机，但他们应该朝着一个共同的目标努力，那就是增加营业额，从而最终带来员工福利。 The News API can help you to analyze market trends and give you concrete data to analyze and create your comprehensive marketing strategy. News API可以帮助您分析市场趋势，并为您提供具体数据来分析和制定全面的营销策略。

To locate the most important data reservoirs for your sales intelligence efforts. News API plays a dominant role in gathering news data that is reliable and conclusive to strategize and understand the market.

News API for Sales Intelligence

Newsdata.io is a well-known provider of a News API that makes it easier to get news data from all over the world. It is an invaluable tool that can be used to directly extract news data about any organization from the vast web.

Historical News: Users can extract historical news data as per their requirements for up to 5 years. The quotation of history repeats itself is quite extensively based on market behaviors, and keeping track of past data helps the users to the nature of the market more extensively.

Conclusion
Sales and turnover are things every organization craves and aims for. Every organization and the people working in it have different end goals and motives, but they should work towards one common goal, which is to increase turnover, which ultimately results in employee benefits. The News API can help you to analyze market trends and give you concrete data to analyze and create your comprehensive marketing strategy.

Items.Title: 如何在 React / Next JS 中创建自定义 Hook | How To Create Custom Hooks in React / Next JS
Items.Description:
它们提供了一种更简洁且可重用的方式来管理组件逻辑。 一些流行的例子是 useState 和 useEffect Hooks。

先决条件

  • 对 useState 和 useEffect 等 React js 钩子有很好的理解。

  • 您有一个正在运行的 React 项目，您想要为其创建自定义挂钩。

为什么我们应该使用自定义钩子？

您编写了一段代码，后来发现其他文件可能需要该逻辑，一个好的做法是将逻辑抽象到自定义挂钩中。

例如，您编写了一个允许您与 API 通信的功能，此逻辑包括返回错误、返回成功消息以及向 API 发送数据。 将此逻辑抽象为挂钩始终是一个好习惯，这样您就可以在代码库中重用它，这样您就不必在需要该逻辑的每个文件中重写它。 这有助于使您的代码更有条理，并且减少重复逻辑的体积。 一个干净的代码原则是 DRY，即不要重复，这意味着如果某个代码在一个文件中多次使用，您可以将其转换为单个代码块或文件，然后从该文件中导出以在应用程序中使用。

创建自定义钩子时需要注意以下一些规则

  1. 切勿在循环、条件或嵌套函数内调用 Hook。

  2. 始终在 React 组件的顶层使用 Hooks。

  3. 切勿从常规 JavaScript 函数中调用 Hook。

  4. 相反，从 React 功能组件调用 Hooks 或从自定义 Hooks 调用 Hooks。

  5. 使用“use”前缀来命名您的函数和文件名，例如：useEffect、useState、useFetch。

我们会做什么？

我们将利用JSON Placeholder Fake Data API来获取我们将用于应用程序的虚拟数据。

与 API 交互时，我们需要一个库来与该 API 交互，为此我们将使用Axios 。

安装 Axios 使用 NPM 时，在终端上运行此命令。

npm install axios

使用 Yarn 时，请在终端上运行此命令。

yarn add axios

在您的 React 应用程序上创建一个Home.js文件并粘贴以下代码

// Home.js import { useState, useEffect } from "react"; import axios from "axios"; const Home = () => { const [data, setData] = useState(null); const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(null); const [error, setError] = useState(null); useEffect(() => { handleGetData(); }, []); const handleGetData = async () => { setIsLoading(true); try { const getData = await axios.get( "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/todos" ); if (getData.status == "200") { setData(getData.data); setIsLoading(false); } } catch (error) { setError(error); throw console.error(error); } }; return ( <> {isLoading && <p>Loading...</p>} {error && ( <div> There was an Error <button onClick={handleGetData}>Try Again</button>{" "} </div> )} {data && !isLoading && data.map((item) => { return <p key={item.id}>{item.title}</p>; })} </> ); }; export default Home;

你应该在网页上看到这个

这个 Home.js 文件做了 6 件基本的事情

  1. 从 JSON Placeholder API 获取数据。

  2. 将响应数据设置为状态和加载状态

  3. 捕获文件上的任何错误并将其设置为错误状态。

  4. 显示获取数据以供用户查看时是否出现错误。

  5. 显示加载状态供用户查看。

  6. 显示数据供用户查看

这是查询 API 的页面的基本功能，因此我们需要抽象此逻辑。 在典型的 API 上，您将拥有不同的端点，例如 /createAccount 或 /getUser，并且要实现它，您必须为所有文件重写此逻辑。 因此，让我们将其抽象为可重用的逻辑。

在 JSON Placeholder API 上，我们可以访问这些路由，因此允许我们的自定义挂钩接受任何后缀链接是有意义的

步骤 1：确定要重用的代码。

最好将执行类似逻辑的文件放入同一文件夹中，您应该有一个 hooks 文件夹来保存将为项目创建的所有自定义挂钩。 因此创建一个 hooks 文件夹和一个名为 useRequest.js 的文件

你的目录应该看起来像这样。

 -src --\hooks\useRequest.js

让我们将必要的可重用逻辑从 index.js 文件复制到useRequest.js文件中，该文件应如下所示。

 // useRequest.js import { useState } from "react"; import axios from "axios"; const useRequest = () => { const [data, setData] = useState(null); const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(null); const [error, setError] = useState(null); const handleGetData = async () => { setIsLoading(true); try { const getData = await axios.get( "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/todos" ); if (getData.status == "200") { setData(getData.data); setIsLoading(false); } } catch (error) { setError(error); throw console.error(error); } }; return { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData }; }; export default useRequest;

代码解释

 const [data, setData] = useState(null); const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(null); const [error, setError] = useState(null);

这些状态将处理 API 的响应、加载状态和错误状态。

 const handleGetData = async () => { setIsLoading(true); try { const getData = await axios.get( "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/todos" ); if (getData.status == "200") { setData(getData.data); setIsLoading(false); } else{ setError("There was an error"); } } catch (error) { setError(error); throw console.error(error); } };

它首先将加载状态设置为 true，因为我们希望在钩子调用端点时显示加载状态。

  • 我们将主要逻辑包装在 try/catch 块中，因为我们想要处理可能遇到的错误，例如网络错误、请求超时或数据未找到错误。

  • 下一行使用axios调用 API，检查状态是否为 200（即有数据响应），并将数据和加载状态设置为 false，如果错误状态不是 200，则表示有错误，所以我们处理这个问题。

    •  return { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData };

    在这里，我们公开了 API 调用（数据）的响应、加载和错误状态，以及我们之前解释过的 handleGetData 函数。

    第 2 步：如何使用

    由于我们已经将可重用功能抽象为钩子，因此以下是如何使用它。

     const { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData } = useRequest();

    只要看一下这个，您就会发现它与使用基本的 React hook（如 useState 或 useEffect）非常相似。

    这是我们的Home.js文件现在的样子

    // Home.js import { useState, useEffect } from "react"; import useRequest from "../../hooks/useRequest"; const Home = () => { const { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData } = useRequest(); useEffect(() => { handleGetData(); }, []); return ( <div> {isLoading && <p>Loading...</p>} {error && ( <div> There was an Error <button onClick={handleGetData}>Try Again</button>{" "} </div> )} {data && !isLoading && data.map((item) => { return <p key={item.id}>{item.title}</p>; })} </div> ); }; export default Home;

    我们可以看到，我们已经将代码减少到了 30 行以下，这是一件好事，我们已经将调用 API 的逻辑抽象为自定义钩子，我们将使其更具可重用性现在。

    如果您已经读到这里，您已经了解了在 React 中创建自定义钩子的基础知识，我们在下一节中要做的就是使钩子接受任何端点和基本 API 链接。

    第三步：使挂钩更易于重复使用。

    我们在这里要做的是允许 useRequest.js 函数接受两个参数，即我们的BASE_API是我们可以在所有端点上找到的最常见的 URL。

    例如BASE_API: www.simpleapi.com ， suffix: /posts ，因此完整的 URL 看起来像www.simpleapi.com/posts 。

    以下是我们将对 useRequest.js 进行的更改

    在 Home.js 文件中，将const { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData } = useRequest()替换为const useRequest = (BASE_API, suffix) => { ...... }这仅显示接受BASE_API和suffix.的函数suffix.

    在 useRequest.js 中替换const getData = await axios.get("https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/todos"); 与const getData = await axios.get(BASE_API + suffix);

    为 Axios 实现此功能以正确获取BASE_API和suffix端点。

    我们最终的 useRequest.js 文件如下所示。

     // useRequest.js import { useState } from "react"; import axios from "axios"; const useRequest = (BASE_API, suffix) => { const [data, setData] = useState(null); const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(null); const [error, setError] = useState(null); const handleGetData = async () => { setIsLoading(true); try { const getData = await axios.get(BASE_API + suffix); if (getData.status == "200") { setData(getData.data); setIsLoading(false); } else { setError("There was an error"); } } catch (error) { setError(error); throw console.error(error); } }; return { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData }; }; export default useRequest;

    一个好的做法是将 BASE_API 端点保留在 .env 文件上，并将后缀保留在要使用它的文件上。

    例如，假设我们想要创建一个评论页面，我们需要做的就是创建const suffix=" /comments "并像这样使用它

    // DisplayComment.js const BASE_API = "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com"; const suffix = "/comments"; const { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData } = useRequest( BASE_API, suffix );

    结论

    在本教程中，我们创建了一个 React hook 来处理与 API 的自定义交互，我们这样做是因为我们希望将功能抽象到一个单独的文件中。 这有助于我们模块化代码并确保我们不会在多个文件中重复相同的代码。

    如果您发现本文有帮助，请点赞或发表评论，与可能需要本文的朋友/网络分享，并在Linkedin 、 Twitter和hashnode上与我联系。



    原文:

    React Hooks allows developers "hook into" React's lifecycle and state management features from functional components. They provide a more concise and reusable way to manage component logic. Some popular examples are useState and useEffect Hooks.

    Prerequisites

    • Good understanding of react js hooks like useState and useEffect.

    • You have a react project running that you want to create a custom hook for.

    Why should we use custom hooks?

    React Hooks helps us to reuse logic across our code base. You've written a piece of Code that you later found out other files that might need that logic, a good practice is to abstract that logic into a custom hook.

    For example, you've written a functionality that allows you to communicate with an API, this logic includes, returning errors, returning the success messages, and sending data to the API. It's always a good practice to abstract this logic into a hook so you can reuse it across your codebase so that you don't have to rewrite it in every file you need that logic. This helps to make your code more organized and less bulky with repeated logic. One clean Code principle is DRY ie Do not Repeat Yourself, which means if a code is used multiple times across a file, you turn it into a single code block or into a file and export it from that file to be used across your application.

    Here are some rules you need to take note of when creating custom hooks

    1. Never call Hooks inside loops, conditions, or nested functions.

    2. Always use Hooks at the top level of your React component.

    3. Never call Hooks from regular JavaScript functions.

    4. Instead, call Hooks from React functional components or call Hooks from custom Hooks.

    5. Make use of the "use" prefix to name your functions and the filename eg: useEffect, useState, useFetch.

    What are we going to do?

    We're going to utilize JSON Placeholder Fake Data API to fetch dummy data that we are going to use for our application.

    When interacting with an API, we would need a library to interact with that API, for that we are going to use Axios for this.

    To install Axios When using NPM, run this command on your terminal.

    npm install axios

    When using Yarn, run this command on your terminal.

    yarn add axios

    Create a Home.js file on your react app and paste the code below

    // Home.js import { useState, useEffect } from "react";
import axios from "axios"; const Home = () => { const [data, setData] = useState(null); const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(null); const [error, setError] = useState(null); useEffect(() => { handleGetData(); }, []); const handleGetData = async () => { setIsLoading(true); try { const getData = await axios.get( "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/todos" ); if (getData.status == "200") { setData(getData.data); setIsLoading(false); } } catch (error) { setError(error); throw console.error(error); } }; return ( <> {isLoading && <p>Loading...</p>}
 {error && ( <div> There was an Error <button onClick={handleGetData}>Try Again</button>{" "}
 </div>
 )} {data && !isLoading && data.map((item) => { return <p key={item.id}>{item.title}</p>;
 })} </>
 );
}; export default Home;

    you should see this on the web page

    This Home.js File does 6 basic things

    1. Fetching the data from JSON Placeholder API.

    2. Set that response data to a state and a loading state

    3. Catches any error on the file and sets it to an error state.

    4. Displays if there was an error getting the data for the user to see.

    5. Shows the loading state for the user to see.

    6. Displays the Data for the user to see

    This is the basic functionality for pages that query an API hence the need for us to abstract this logic. On a typical API you would have different endpoints like /createAccount or /getUser and to implement it you'll have to rewrite this logic for all files. So let's abstract this into a reusable logic.

    On the JSON Placeholder API, we have access to these routes, so it would make sense for us to allow our custom hook to accept any suffix link

    Step 1: Identify the Code to be reused.

    It's good practice to put files that are performing similar logic into the same folder, you should have a hooks folder to hold all the custom hooks you'll create for your project. So create a hooks folder and a file called useRequest.js

    Your directory should look something like this.

    -src
--\hooks\useRequest.js

    Let's copy the necessary re-useable logic from our index.js file into our useRequest.js file, which should look like this.

    // useRequest.js import { useState } from "react";
import axios from "axios"; const useRequest = () => { const [data, setData] = useState(null); const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(null); const [error, setError] = useState(null); const handleGetData = async () => { setIsLoading(true); try { const getData = await axios.get( "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/todos" ); if (getData.status == "200") { setData(getData.data); setIsLoading(false); } } catch (error) { setError(error); throw console.error(error); } }; return { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData };
}; export default useRequest;

    Explanation of the code 

     const [data, setData] = useState(null); const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(null); const [error, setError] = useState(null);

    These are the states that would handle the response from the API, the loading state, and the error state.

     const handleGetData = async () => { setIsLoading(true); try { const getData = await axios.get( "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/todos" ); if (getData.status == "200") { setData(getData.data); setIsLoading(false); } else{ setError("There was an error"); } } catch (error) { setError(error); throw console.error(error); } };

    handleGetData is an async function, here's what it does.

    1. It first sets the loading state to true, because we want to show a loading state while our hook is calling the endpoint.

    2. We're wrapping the main logic in a try/catch block because we want to handle the errors we might get, errors such as network errors, a request timeout or a data not found error.

    3. The next line calls the API with axios, checks if the status is 200(ie, there was a response with data), and sets the data and loading state to false, if the error state is not 200, it means there was an error, so we handle that.

    return { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData };

    The return statement is important because you might not always want to make all the functions or variables accessible on your hook, so the return allows us to specify what we want to expose. Here we are exposing the response of our API call(data), loading, and error states, and handleGetData function which we explained earlier.

    Step 2: How to use it

    Since we're done abstracting the reusable functionality into a hook, here's how to use it.

     const { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData } = useRequest();

    Just looking at this, you'll see that it's pretty similar to how you'll use the basic react hooks like useState or useEffect.

    Here's how our Home.js file now looks

    // Home.js import { useState, useEffect } from "react";
import useRequest from "../../hooks/useRequest"; const Home = () => { const { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData } = useRequest(); useEffect(() => { handleGetData(); }, []); return ( <div> {isLoading && <p>Loading...</p>}
 {error && ( <div> There was an Error <button onClick={handleGetData}>Try Again</button>{" "}
 </div>
 )} {data && !isLoading && data.map((item) => { return <p key={item.id}>{item.title}</p>;
 })} </div>
 );
}; export default Home;

    We can see that we've reduced the code to virtually under 30 lines of code for us, which is a good thing, we've abstracted the logic for calling the API into a custom hook that we are going to make more re-useable now.

    If you've read to this point, you already know the basics of creating custom hooks in React, what we are going to do in the next section is to make the hook accept any endpoint and base API link.

    Step 3: Make the hook more reusable.

    What we are going to do here is to allow our useRequest.js function to accept two arguments, ie. BASE_API and suffix.

    Our BASE_API is the most common url we can find on all our endpoints.

    The suffix is the different endpoints.

    For example BASE_API: www.simpleapi.com, suffix: /posts, so together the full URL would look like www.simpleapi.com/posts.

    Here are the changes we would be making to our useRequest.js

    In your Home.js file replace const { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData } = useRequest() with const useRequest = (BASE_API, suffix) => { ...... } This just shows the function accepting the BASE_API and suffix.

    In your useRequest.js replace const getData = await axios.get("https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/todos"); with const getData = await axios.get(BASE_API + suffix);

    Implement this for Axios to correctly get the BASE_API and suffix endpoint.

    Our final useRequest.js file looks like this.

    // useRequest.js import { useState } from "react";
import axios from "axios"; const useRequest = (BASE_API, suffix) => { const [data, setData] = useState(null); const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(null); const [error, setError] = useState(null); const handleGetData = async () => { setIsLoading(true); try { const getData = await axios.get(BASE_API + suffix); if (getData.status == "200") { setData(getData.data); setIsLoading(false); } else { setError("There was an error"); } } catch (error) { setError(error); throw console.error(error); } }; return { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData };
}; export default useRequest;

    A good practice is to keep your BASE_API endpoint on your .env file and your suffix on the file you want to use it on.

    For example, let's say we want to create a page for comments all we need to do is to create const suffix="/comments" and use it like this

    // DisplayComment.js const BASE_API = "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com"; const suffix = "/comments"; const { data, isLoading, error, handleGetData } = useRequest( BASE_API, suffix );

    Conclusion

    In this tutorial, we created a react hook for handling custom interactions with an API, we did this because we wanted to abstract the functionalities into a separate file. This helps us to modularize our code and ensure that we don't repeat the same code in multiple files.

    If you found this article helpful please like or leave a comment, share it with your friends/network who might need this, and connect with me on Linkedin, Twitter and hashnode.

您是否想在网络世界中实现互联网功能？ 本书将在第五章中介绍如何理解互联网世界的原理。

作为无路线图基础的第五部分。由卡姆兰·艾哈迈德 (Kamran Ahmed) 撰写，我们将编译所有待办事项或后续内容，并以直接和示例的方式进行总结。

互联网启动

在 Guerra Fria 期间，互联网开始了，nos Estados Unidos。 这是美国国家防御部门的研究项目，是 1960 年阿帕网 (ARPANET) 的研究项目。该项目是大学和研究机构之间的信息计算计算机的研究。 一个想法是，在各种重要的时刻，您可以使用不同的方式来实现套管针信息和文档，caso alguma dessas esttruturas（计算机）vise a sofrer algum ataque, ou deixar de funcionar, os outros computadores ainda teria a capacidade de acessar aquele documento,

互联网的发展速度很快，它扩展了学术界的领域，并在全球范围内实现了各种待办事项、联系、管理和组织工作，以及世界各地的门户。

我是你的

没有互联网世界，我们无法使用不同的设备、计算机、智能手机和服务来连接系统，因此可以访问信息和递归。 在互联网上，我们可以通过互联网与其他地方进行联系，可以通过互联网或移动方式进行交流，从而可以进行各种交流。

使用电话示例，例如，您可以使用该设备，也可以使用该设备。 在卡达路，我们可以在卡沃斯的各个角落进行对话，并且可以通过连接方式存在多种方式。

集市、想象中的算法与大系统、关于 estrada 的配置和车辆。 这些配置可以与世界各地的其他人一起使用，环境信息，比较文件，传输视频和其他信息。 通过全球联系、管理和服务，可以进行即时通信和访问以及无限的递归和信息。

互联网通用签证

全球互联网允许在任意连接之间进行通信和传输。 对于功能而言，重要的是要结合基本概念。 您将探索克拉拉形式的概念和简单的方法，以实现该地区的成功。

该协议与英雄相关：互联网协议 (IP) 和传输控制协议 (TCP)。 如果您的 IP 地址与目的地发生冲突，则 TCP 会保证您的地址。

包： Éuma pequena unidade de bados que a Internet envia pelos ares。

  • 代表 uma série de números separados por pontos，como“192.168.1.1”。

    您可以通过“ https://www.google.com ” 进行数字导航，并请求 DNS 服务器、IP 通信。 如果 DNS 服务不提供任何信息，则 DNS 服务可能会遇到 IP 相关问题。 如果服务器与 IP 发生冲突，则通常会与网站连接。

    查看最简单的 IP 和 DNS： support.google.com/a/answer/2573637?hl= …

    在网站中，您可以通过 HTTP 请求服务，并通过专用页面来请求图像、视频等。通过服务器和响应者的工作，可以通过论坛来请求客户。

    请参阅 HTTP 和 HTTPS： www.alura.com.br/artigos/qual-e-diferen…

    门户：São usadas para identificar o applicativo ou service em execução em um dispositivo, cada applicativo ou service recebe um número de porta exclusivo。

  • 套接字：São como fones de ouvido、conectando os endereços IP 和作为应用程序之间专用对话的端口。
  • 连接：São estabelecidas entre dois sockets quando dispositivos desejam se comunicar。 在建立联系的过程中，重要的谈判参数。
  • 转移数据：您可以与罗兰多联系，将数据流插入到元数据包中，保证成功。

    • 有关 TCP/IP 的详细信息： tecnoblog.net/responde/o-que-e-tcp-ip/

    用于保护应用程序的安全。

    证书：São usados para estabelecer a confiança entre cliente e servicevidor。 有关信息，请参阅服务人员的身份以及与其他人进行交流的信息。

  • 握手： 客户和服务商之间的信息处理过程、加密货币和安全参数。
  • 密码传输：用于建立安全连接、使用密码传输和算法以及传输密码以确保客户和服务器的安全。

    • 了解 SSL/TLS 的基本原理： rockcontent.com/br/blog/tls-ssl/

    没有第二次，irei trazer um pouco mais sobre“HTTP e HTTPS”。

    互联网是如何工作的？： cs.fyi/guide/how-does-internet-work

    互联网：IP 地址和 DNS： www.youtube.com/watch? v=5o8CwafCxnU&ab_...

    互联网是如何工作的？： developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn /...

    原文:

    Você está começando no mundo da web e quer entender melhor como a internet funciona? Como você digita um endereço no campo de busca do navegador, e ele te retorna uma página? Pensando nisso decidi criar uma série com cinco artigos introduzindo os principais temas para compreender melhor o mundo da internet.

    Lembrando que todos os assuntos são bem mais complexos do que irei apresentar aqui, a ideia é te dar uma introdução para que você possa ter uma base e conhecer um pouco dos conceitos.

    Todas as cinco partes serão baseadas no roadmap.sh criado por Kamran Ahmed, onde eu irei fazer um compilado de todo o conteúdo e resumir em artigos mais direcionados e exemplificado.

    Iremos começar pelo “How does the internet work? / Como funciona a internet?”.

    Início da Internet

    A internet teve início durante a Guerra Fria, nos Estados Unidos. Como um projeto de pesquisa do Departamento de Defesa dos Estados Unidos, chamado ARPANET, na década de 1960. Foi desenvolvida como uma rede de computadores para compartilhamento de informações entre universidades e instituições de pesquisa. O objetivo era criar uma rede robusta e descentralizada que pudesse resistir a um possível ataque de seus inimigos, e mesmo assim poder transitar as informações sem perigo de perda, permitindo a troca de dados de forma eficiente.

    A ideia é que eles pudessem trocar informações e documentos em vários pontos a qualquer momento, caso alguma dessas estruturas (computador) viesse a sofrer algum ataque, ou deixar de funcionar, os outros computadores ainda teria a capacidade de acessar aquele documento,

    Ao longo do tempo, a internet evoluiu, expandindo-se para além das fronteiras acadêmicas e se tornando uma rede global acessível a todos, conectando pessoas, empresas e organizações em todo o mundo, abrindo as portas para o que nós temos hoje.

    O que é uma rede

    No mundo da internet, uma rede é um sistema de conexão entre diferentes dispositivos, como computadores, smartphones e servidores, que permite a troca de informações e recursos. É como uma teia de conexões que permite que esses dispositivos se comuniquem entre si.

    Uma simples analogia, seria pensar em uma rede de telefones fixos em uma vizinhança. Cada casa tem um telefone e está conectada a outras casas por fios telefônicos. Se alguém em uma casa quer ligar para outra, ele discará o número do telefone daquela casa e a ligação será estabelecida através da rede de fios. Da mesma forma, na internet, os dispositivos estão conectados através de cabos, sinais sem fio ou até mesmo redes móveis, permitindo a comunicação entre eles.

    Usando o exemplo do telefone, é como se cada roteador fosse uma rua, e cada dispositivo fosse uma casa. Em cada rua, nós temos diversas casas que se conversam entre si através de cabos, e existem diversas ruas que se conectam.

    Atualmente, com o avanço da tecnologia e a proliferação de dispositivos conectados, a rede da internet se expandiu globalmente. É como se fosse uma rede mundial interconectada. Agora, podemos imaginar algo como um grande sistema de estradas, onde cada dispositivo é como um veículo na estrada. Esses dispositivos podem se comunicar com outros dispositivos em qualquer parte do mundo, enviando informações, compartilhando arquivos, transmitindo vídeos e muito mais. É uma rede global que conecta pessoas, empresas e serviços, permitindo a comunicação instantânea e o acesso a uma infinidade de recursos e informações.

    Se quiser ler um pouco mais sobre Rede:
    aws.amazon.com/pt/what-is/computer-netw…

    Visão geral da Internet

    A Internet é uma rede global que permite a comunicação e transferência de dados entre dispositivos conectados. Para entender como ela funciona, é importante conhecer alguns conceitos básicos. Vou explorar esses conceitos de forma clara e simples para aqueles que estão começando na área.

    Vamos começar com o básico: a Internet é como uma superestrada que conecta todos os dispositivos do mundo. E o segredo por trás dessa conexão são dois heróis: o Protocolo de Internet (IP) e o Transmission Control Protocol (TCP). O IP é o guia que ajuda os pacotes de dados a encontrarem seu destino, enquanto o TCP garante que esses pacotes cheguem lá.

    Para ter um melhor entendimento é interessante conhecer alguns conceitos que se repetem a todo momento, aqui vai alguns importantes.

    • Pacote: É uma pequena unidade de dados que a Internet envia pelos ares.
    • Roteador: É um dispositivo que direciona os pacotes de dados para o lugar certo, pulando de rede em rede.
    • Endereço IP: É como um RG da Internet, um identificador exclusivo para cada dispositivo conectado.
    • Nome de domínio: É um nome legível usado para identificar um site, como "google.com".
    • DNS: É o sistema responsável por traduzir nomes de domínio em endereços IP.
    • HTTP: É a linguagem dos navegadores e sites, que permite a transferência de dados.
    • HTTPS: É a versão criptografada do HTTP, que garante que a comunicação entre cliente e servidor seja segura e tranquila.
    • SSL/TLS: São protocolos de segurança usados para proteger nossas informações pessoais durante a navegação na Internet.

    IP e DNS

    Um endereço IP é como o endereço de uma casa, é o identificador único do seu dispositivo na Internet. Representado por uma série de números separados por pontos, como "192.168.1.1".

    Os nome de domínio é o apelido charmoso que atribuímos aos sites. O DNS (Domain Name System) entra em ação para traduzir esses apelidos em endereços IP, por exemplo, "google.com" é um nome de domínio.

    O fluxo que ocorre nos bastidores da internet é o seguinte:

    Quando você digita um domínio na barra de busca do seu navegador, como por exemplo "https://www.google.com", o navegador envia uma solicitação a um servidor DNS, perguntando se ele tem o IP correspondente àquele domínio. Caso o servidor DNS não possua essa informação, ele busca em outros servidores DNS até encontrar o IP correto. Assim que o servidor encontra o IP, ele o retorna ao usuário para que possa se conectar ao site desejado.

    Se quiser ler um pouco mais sobre IP e DNS: support.google.com/a/answer/2573637?hl=…

    HTTP e HTTPS

    HTTP (Hypertext Transfer Protocol) e HTTPS (HTTP Secure) são dois dos protocolos mais usados em aplicativos e serviços baseados na Internet.

    HTTP é o protocolo usado para transferir dados entre um cliente (navegador) e um servidor (site). Ao entrar em um site, o seu navegador realiza uma solicitação HTTP para o servidor, essa solicitação pode ser a própria página ou recursos como imagens, vídeos, etc. O trabalho do servidor é responder, com os recursos que foram solicitados pelo cliente.

    HTTPS é uma versão mais segura do HTTP, que criptografa os dados transmitidos entre o cliente e o servidor usando SSL/TLS, com isso temos uma camada extra em questão de segurança, já que essa comunicação é realizada com a criptografia SSL/TLS, protegendo dados sensíveis, como é mostrado na imagem.

    Se quiser ler um pouco mais sobre HTTP e HTTPS: www.alura.com.br/artigos/qual-e-diferen…

    TCP/IP

    O TCP/IP (Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol) é um protocolo de comunicação utilizado pela maior parte dos aplicativos e serviços que nós temos hoje na internet, focando na entrega de dados confiáveis, ordenadas e com verificação de erros entre aplicativos executados em diferentes dispositivos.

    Termos e conceitos chaves para entender melhor o TCP/IP:

    • Portas: São usadas para identificar o aplicativo ou serviço em execução em um dispositivo, cada aplicativo ou serviço recebe um número de porta exclusivo.
    • Sockets: São como fones de ouvido, conectando os endereços IP e as portas para uma conversa exclusiva entre aplicativos.
    • Conexões: São estabelecidas entre dois sockets quando dispositivos desejam se comunicar. Durante o processo de estabelecimento da conexão, são negociados parâmetros importantes.
    • Transferência de dados: Uma vez que a conexão está rolando, os dados fluem entre os aplicativos em pacotes com metadados, garantindo uma entrega de sucesso.

    Se quiser ler um pouco mais sobre TCP/IP: tecnoblog.net/responde/o-que-e-tcp-ip/

    SSL/TLS

    SSL/TLS é um protocolo usado para criptografar os nossos dados que são transmitidos pela Internet. É usado para fornecer conexões seguras para aplicativos.

    Termos e conceitos chaves para entender melhor o SSL/TLS:

    • Certificados: São usados para estabelecer a confiança entre cliente e servidor. Contêm informações sobre a identidade do servidor e são assinados por terceiros confiáveis.
    • Handshake: É o processo de troca de informações entre cliente e servidor para negociar a criptografia e outros parâmetros para a conexão segura.
    • Criptografia: Após a conexão segura ser estabelecida, os dados são criptografados usando o algoritmo acordado e transmitidos com segurança entre cliente e servidor.

    Se quiser ler um pouco mais sobre SSL/TLS: rockcontent.com/br/blog/tls-ssl/

    Dessa maneira chegamos ao fim desse artigo, falamos um pouco sobre alguns tópicos de como a internet é formada, o objetivo era apenas trazer alguns conceitos para que nós possamos nos aprofundar futuramente. No segundo artigo, irei trazer um pouco mais sobre “HTTP e HTTPS”.

    Fonte:

    How does the Internet Work?: cs.fyi/guide/how-does-internet-work

    The Internet: IP Addresses & DNS: www.youtube.com/watch?v=5o8CwafCxnU&ab_…

    How does the Internet work?: developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/…

    História da internet:
    pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hist%C3%B3ria_da_…

    O surgimento da internet: www.fatosdesconhecidos.com.br/o-surgime…

哈雷·威尔瑟姆

发表于 6 月 27 日

您是一位正在寻找价格实惠的移动应用程序开发服务的初创企业家，还是准备好加快您的产品进入市场的时间？ 关于Flutter的进步，你是对的。 自 2020 年以来，尖端开发公司增加了 Flutter 的可用性，以创建经济实惠的移动解决方案。 特别是在跨平台移动应用程序开发方面，Flutter 无疑是一个绝佳的选择。

我们进行了广泛的研究，编制了排名前 5 的Flutter 应用开发公司名单。 我们的排名系统基于位置、小时费率、最低项目预算、投资组合和客户评级等因素。 然后我们根据这些数据比较并列出排名前 5 位的 Flutter 应用开发公司。

美国最佳 5 Flutter 应用开发公司

他们拥有经过认证的 Flutter 开发人员，他们在 Flutter 应用程序开发方面拥有出色的技能。

Items.Title: 为什么人们应该选择质量保证而不是其他保证？ | Why should one choose quality assurance over the others?
Items.Description:
全球软件开发活动市场随着先进的技术框架和高端技术而不断发展。 因此，IT 和软件团队显然需要投入战术努力和战略措施来制定明确的质量保证策略。 质量保证的实施可确保使用特定的质量标准来构建软件产品或应用程序。 在本文中，您将了解质量保证的战略重要性。

什么是质量保证(QA)？

它专注于提供长期的客户体验。 提供有关客户的需求、需求和满意度的具体而深入的见解。 它针对需要改进的具体领域提供战略指导，增强与质量相关的服务并定制支持策略。 除此之外，质量保证还有助于提高员工提高绩效水平的积极性。

质量保证工作场景：

质量保证流程可确保构建的最终产品满足客户的特定要求，从而减少软件中的错误数量。 从用户体验角度出现的任何问题都将由 QA 测试人员进行策略性识别。 软件经过测试后，QA 测试人员将记录具体问题，然后将报告发送给开发团队，以便他们修改软件。

此开发和测试周期将持续下去，直到 QA 分析师（或 QA 分析师团队）对最终产品感到满意为止，最后提供批准印章。 一旦产品得到 QA 分析师的认可，就可以发布。 除了自动化工具外，还可以根据项目范围和要求使用移动测试工具来提高质量。

以下是描述质量保证的战略重要性的几个关键因素：

  1. 可以友好地满足客户的需求：每当构建软件产品或应用程序时，QA 团队都会确保不惜任何代价不损害用户体验。 当谈到企业软件时，组织寻求能够加速其流程并解决业务效率低下的解决方案。 如果软件出现故障并且生产力不高，那么组织价值可能会受到影响。

  2. 设定正确的标准：当敏捷方法最佳实践纳入质量保证策略时，团队可以改进对改进能力的搜索，并且也将有更多时间进行探索性测试。 在当今快节奏、竞争激烈的IT市场环境中，质量保证平台发挥着举足轻重的作用。 为了确保移动应用程序的质量，可以根据项目范围和要求使用移动测试工具。

  3. 在开发过程的早期发现并修复问题：在软件开发生命周期过程中检测软件错误是显而易见的。 但是，如果在软件开发过程的后期阶段检测并修复这些错误，问题可能会升级。 这反过来会导致匆忙修复并不可避免地造成延迟，从而影响开发人员的资源。

因此，应该有一个明确的测试实施策略和有组织的测试流程，有助于战略性地分配资源并在成本上升之前进行识别。

  1. 建立客户的信任：如果构建高质量的产品，就可以建立长期有效的业务关系。 这在制作 B2B 产品时非常相关。 当质量得到适当的优先考虑并为客户创造价值时，就能反过来提高盈利能力。 从长远来看，产品和服务的可持续性和稳定性可以得到简化。

结论：如果您希望为您的特定项目实施 QA 测试，那么请在线访问一家全球知名的软件测试服务公司，该公司将为您提供符合您的项目需求的实用测试框架。

原文:

The global market for software development activities is evolving constantly with advanced technological frameworks and high-end technologies. Hence, it becomes obvious for the IT and software team to put in tactical efforts and strategic measures to come up with a well-defined quality assurance strategy. The implementation of quality assurance ensures that specific quality standards are utilized to build a software product or application. In this article, you will get to know the strategic importance of quality assurance.

What is Quality Assurance (QA)?

QA is a structured process that ensures that the business results are aligned with business goals. It focuses on delivering long-term customer experience. Specific and deep insights are provided about the customer's wants, needs and satisfaction. It provides strategic guidance on the specific areas that need to be improved, enhancing the quality-related services and customizing the support strategies. Apart from these, Quality assurance also helps in boosting the motivation of employees for scaling up their performance levels.

The working scenario of Quality Assurance:

The quality assurance process makes sure that the final product that has been built meets the client specific requirements and thus the number of errors is reduced in the software. Any issues arising from the user's experience perspective will be tactically identified by QA testers. Once the software has been tested, specific problems will be documented by a QA tester and then the report is sent to the development team so that they can revise the software.

This development and testing cycle will continue until the QA analyst (or team of QA analysts) is content with the final product and then finally, the stamp of approval is provided. Once the product has been signed off by the QA analyst then it can be launched. Apart from automation tools, mobile testing tools can be used depending on the project scope and requirements to enhance the quality.

Following are a few key factors that describe the strategic importance of Quality assurance:

  1. Customer's demands can be met amicably: Whenever a software product or application is being built, the QA team ensures that user experience should not be compromised at any cost. When it comes to enterprise software, organizations go for solutions that can accelerate their processes and solve their business inefficiencies. If the software has glitches and is not that productive, then the organizational value can get impacted.

  2. Setting the right standards: When agile methodology best practices are incorporated in a quality assurance strategy, then the team can improve the search for improvement capabilities and there will also be more time for exploratory testing. In today's fast-paced and competitive IT market scenario, the platform of quality assurance plays a pivotal role. For ensuring the quality of mobile applications, mobile testing tools can be used based on project scope and requirements.

  3. Issues are detected and fixed early on in the development process: Detecting software bugs in the Software Development Life Cycle process is evident. However, issues can escalate if these bugs are detected and fixed in the later stages of the software development process. This will in turn lead to hurried fixes and inevitably cause delays and thus impact developers' resources.

For this reason, there should be a well-defined test implementation strategy and organized testing processes that can help in allocating resources strategically and identifying before the costs get escalated.

  1. Customer's trust is built: Business relationships can be made long-term and effective if a high-quality product is being built. This is highly relevant when B2B products are being made. When the quality is properly prioritized and value is generated for customers then it can in turn lead to increased profitability. In the long-run the sustainability and stability of the products and services can be streamlined.

Conclusion: If you are looking forward to implementing QA testing for your specific project, then do visit online a globally acclaimed software testing services company that will provide you with a pragmatic testing framework that is in line with your project needs.
